title: Advertising Agencies

narrative.executive_view: 541810 covers businesses that create campaigns and place advertising, offering creative, account-management, production, media-planning and buying capabilities [S1]. This makes it highly AI-exposed, but that exposure does not create an easy roll-up: labor-based fees and procurement can pass cost savings to clients, while in-housing and platform automation challenge the product itself. The screen therefore gives meaningful task automation but low retained capture and a conservative small-agency multiple bridge.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Generative tools can reduce first-draft copy, asset versions, production design, research synthesis, media plans, reporting, billing support and project coordination. Client judgment, brand accountability, senior selling and relationship continuity remain human-intensive. The supplied 5418 role mix is a four-digit proxy contaminated by media representatives, display and promotional occupations; judgments explicitly discount those lines and use a bounded residual for account service, production and administrative roles absent from the top ten [S1].
narrative.value_capture: ANA reports that labor-based fees remained the dominant agency-compensation form, especially among smaller advertisers, even as fixed/output fees gained use [S3]. Under labor-based scopes, AI hours saved invite procurement repricing rather than pure agency margin retention. Output fees can retain a spread, but that benefit is offset by platforms and in-house teams becoming cheaper substitutes. This is why pi_dist is low and separate from the still-real value of a strong client relationship or specialist positioning.
narrative.adoption_timing: BTOS evidence reports 18% of firms using AI in a business function in late 2025/early 2026, with high use in knowledge-intensive professional services but limited functional breadth among adopters [S2]. Advertising's task profile should progress faster than the economy-wide average, but effective adoption includes workflow, approvals, brand risk and client billing, not merely tool use. Three years to 50% effective operational adoption is a judgment rather than a claim that 541810 already has 50% adoption.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The frozen dataset estimates 1,175 firms in the $1–10M EBITDA band, largely 20–99-employee agencies. JEGI reports that digital-marketing assets with £4m–£7.5m EBITDA traded at a 10.8x median, while larger assets received still higher multiples [S5]. That supports real deal interest but is neither a U.S. 541810 series nor a comp for $1–4M EBITDA agencies. A 5.5x entry and 7.0x exit reflect a deliberate discount for smaller scale, client concentration, owner dependence and normalized-EBITDA uncertainty.
narrative.terminal_demand: The paid product is pressured, not automatically melting. Agencies retain value where they provide brand judgment, accountable coordination, specialist vertical knowledge and integrated execution; those services sit within the exact NAICS definition [S1]. But ANA reports 82% of members had an in-house agency in 2023 and the cited research reports meaningful internal cost and time advantages [S4]. AI-native creative and platform automation therefore put durable pressure on undifferentiated production and media execution.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The key risks are client concentration, principal-led revenue, labor-fee repricing, in-housing and boundary leakage into PR, media buying, marketing consulting and design. The available multiple evidence covers digital marketing and larger assets, not exact NAICS 541810 lower-middle-market transactions [S5]. The target screen should be underwritten on net revenue rather than gross media billings, normalized owner compensation, churn and contracts; no generic agency statistic substitutes for that diligence.

sources.title: NAICS 541810 Advertising Agencies definition
sources.what_it_supports: The exact 541810 scope, included services and exclusions for media buying, design, marketing consulting and media representatives.

sources.title: The Microstructure of AI Diffusion: Evidence From Firms, Business Functions, and Worker Tasks
sources.what_it_supports: National BTOS AI-use levels, higher knowledge-intensive sector adoption and limited functional breadth among adopting firms.

sources.title: ANA: Labor-Based Fees Remain Dominant Form Of Agency Compensation
sources.what_it_supports: Agency-compensation structure, including predominance of labor-based fees and small-advertiser use of labor-based agreements.

sources.title: ANA In-House Agencies research summary
sources.what_it_supports: In-house agency penetration and reported cost/time efficiency evidence; this is advertiser-member evidence, not a small-agency revenue survey.

sources.title: JEGI CLARITY notes increased M&A activity within digital marketing services
sources.what_it_supports: Directional marketing-services M&A activity and the reported 10.8x median for £4m–£7.5m EBITDA digital-marketing assets.

sources.title: Marketing Agency EBITDA Multiples & Valuations — 2025
sources.what_it_supports: A secondary agency-valuation reference describing 8–12x top-end sales for high-growth, low-concentration agencies; used only as a non-comparable ceiling reference.

labor_share.method: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $33,005.0M / sectoral output (T30) $68,557.8M, year 2022
sources: BLS Industry Productivity and Costs flat files (ip.data.1.AllData), measures L02 and T30

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $303,923 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541810); class <5: 9,620 firms, avg revenue/firm $521,704 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $81,647 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,690 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,849,936 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $289,515 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,101 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,849,742 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $602,485 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 977 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,049,911 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,885,811 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 198 firms, avg revenue/firm $48,263,556 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,553,246 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 96 firms, avg revenue/firm $267,062,250 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $41,795,242 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,175
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Generative production, research, reporting and coordination are materially exposed, while client leadership, brand accountability and sales remain partially human.
caveats: The supplied 4-digit 5418 wage mix includes sibling advertising industries, so the role allocation is an agency-specific adjustment rather than a direct 541810 observation.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Pitch preparation and CRM work automate; relationship-led selling persists.
Draft content and monitoring automate; media relationships persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Research synthesis, reporting and planning are highly AI-exposed.
Resourcing and reporting automate; accountable leadership remains.
Production design and versioning are directly exposed; art judgment persists.
This is mostly sibling-code contamination and is assigned low agency relevance.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Sales support automates but business development remains relationship-led.
This is mainly display-advertising contamination, not an agency core role.
Analysis and internal work automate; strategy and client leadership persist.
Creative concepts and production accelerate, while direction and approval remain human.
Unlisted account service, traffic, production and admin have high task exposure but retain client coordination.

pi_dist.rationale: Labor-based fees and procurement make much productivity savings contestable; output-based work can retain more.
caveats: ANA's compensation evidence is marketer-survey based and does not give an exact small-agency revenue-weighted mix.

pi_moat.rationale: Integrated client relationships and specialist judgment can persist, but in-housing and platform tools reduce switching friction for generic agencies.
caveats: In-house penetration is advertiser-member evidence and may not map to SMB clients of lower-middle-market agencies.

t50_years.rationale: Advertising's information and creative workflows are more AI-ready than the average firm, but effective adoption includes approvals, quality control and commercial workflow changes.
caveats: BTOS does not publish a direct 541810 effective-adoption series.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: BTOS reports 18% of firms used AI in a business function in the cited national reference period.
caveats: The observed statistic is national, not an exact advertising-agency rate.

historical_analogs.value: Digital production, self-serve ad platforms and in-housing previously shifted execution work toward clients and platforms while retaining specialist strategy work.
historical_analogs.rationale: In-housing evidence supplies a directional analogue; this is not a quantified causal estimate.
caveats: The analogy does not prove the pace or magnitude of generative-AI substitution.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Use a neutral founder-led professional-services planning assumption for the lower-middle-market target set.
caveats: No NAICS- and EBITDA-band-specific owner-age source was located; confirm seller and management succession case by case.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No industry-specific source establishing a succession shortage was identified.
caveats: This deliberately withholds the succession uplift.

active_consolidators.rationale: Use a directional small count of relevant digital-marketing acquirers given documented M&A activity, rather than an asserted 541810 buyer census.
caveats: JEGI's source covers digital marketing services broadly and not a complete count of 541810 buyers.

buy_mult.rationale: Discount the reported larger digital-marketing comparables for $1–10M EBITDA size, owner dependence, client concentration and agency boundary differences.
caveats: Neither source is a direct U.S. 541810 $1–10M EBITDA transaction series; buy multiple must be validated with live brokered comps and QoE.

exit_mult.rationale: Assume only a modest premium from scaled operations, recurring revenue and lower concentration; remain below non-comparable larger-asset references.
caveats: Exit multiple depends on net revenue, client retention, data/technology differentiation and normalized owner compensation; it is not an observed agency median.

pricing_structure.value: Predominantly labor-based fee scopes with some fixed/output-based projects; AI savings face client procurement repricing.
pricing_structure.rationale: ANA describes labor-based fees as dominant and fixed/output fee use as growing.
caveats: Compensation structures vary by client size and agency type; media billings must be separated from net revenue.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A high task-exposure judgment and three-year adoption path are consistent because generative production is ready before all commercial workflows are converted.

terminal_value.justification: Integrated agency judgment survives, but in-housing, generative production and platform automation pressure paid demand for undifferentiated execution.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No: the supplied 5418 wage mix and the digital-marketing deal data are disclosed proxies rather than exact small 541810 observations.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Yes: labor-based scopes and procurement can pass AI productivity gains to clients, while output pricing may partly offset the effect.

dataset_mismatch.description: The role mix is 4-digit 5418 and includes media, display and promotional sibling-code occupations rather than a clean 541810 agency mix.
sensitivity: A true-agency account-service and creative mix can change replaceable share.
Gross media billings must not be confused with net revenue or EBITDA.

subsegments: Specialist creative, production and brand agencies
market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Full-service and digital agencies serving small and mid-sized advertisers
market_boundary.selection_basis: Matches the Census definition and lower-middle-market target population; no measured code-level revenue share is claimed.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A ranges from 5.0 to 2.0 and materially changes S.
Fee-model mix determines whether productivity becomes margin or client price reduction.
The modest M spread is sensitive to concentration and agency differentiation.

reviewer_note: Reopened every listed score-driving source on 2026-07-21. Underwrite exact NAICS scope, net revenue, client concentration, owner normalization, contract durability and fee structure at target level.
